steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: “students can sign up ______ the world-famous Meisner acting technique.” The blank follows the phrasal verb ‘sign up’ and precedes a noun phrase. The standard construction ‘sign up to [infinitive]’ expresses the purpose of signing up.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "to learn": the infinitive ‘to learn’ correctly follows ‘sign up’ to express purpose: ‘sign up to learn the technique.’ ✓
  • "learned": past tense; cannot follow ‘sign up’ to form a grammatical or meaningful phrase. ✗
  • "are learning": present progressive; placing it after ‘sign up’ creates an ungrammatical construction. ✗
  • "learn": the bare infinitive without ‘to’ does not follow ‘sign up’ in standard English; the preposition ‘to’ is required. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
